titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Gerald Robert Vizenor Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday November 16, 2018 at 04:03
Transformed with yale.addEadidUrl.xsl. Adds @url with handle for finding aid. Overwrites @url if already present.

did.unittitle: Gerald Robert Vizenor papers

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection consists of writings, correspondence, research and teaching material, photographs, printed material, audiovisual material, computer disks, and other papers, documenting the literary and academic career of Gerald Robert Vizenor and relating to many of his published works on Native American literature, history, and culture.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Ken Lopez Bookseller on various funds, 2000-2008.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Box 44 and 102 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 45, 47 (reel-to-reel recording, video and film reels): Restricted Fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 46, 80 (computer disks): Restricted Fragile. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Gerald Robert Vizenor Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Gerald Robert Vizenor Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, and competing priorities. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and in some instances minimal organization, at or around the time of receipt by the library. Further description was carried out in 2011. Various acquisitions associated with the collection have not been merged and organized as a whole. Each acquisition is described separately in the contents list below, titled according to month and year of acquisition.

As a rule, descriptive information found in the Collection Contents section is drawn in large part from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This collection includes material formerly classed as: Uncat MSS 71, Uncat MSS 701, and Uncat MSS 1029.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

bioghist.head: Gerald Robert Vizenor (1934)
bioghist.p: Gerald Robert Vizenor is a Native American author, poet, and teacher. His works of prose comprise both fiction and nonfiction, and include The People Named the Chippewa: Narrative Histories (1984), Bearheart: The Heirship Chronicles (1990; originally published as Darkness in Saint Louis Bearheart in 1978), The Heirs of Columbus (1991), and Manifest Manners: Postindian Warriors of Survivance (1994), among others. Vizenor is the founder and editor of the American Indian Literature and Critical Studies series at the University of Oklahoma Press.

Vizenor was born in 1934, and is an enrolled member of the Minnesota Chippewa Tribe, White Earth Reservation. He graduated from the University of Minnesota in 1960, and is Professor of American Studies at the University of New Mexico, and Professor Emeritus at the University of California, Berkeley.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of writings, correspondence, research and teaching material, photographs, printed material, audiovisual material, computer disks, and other papers, documenting the literary and academic career of Gerald Robert Vizenor and relating to many of his published works on Native American literature, history, and culture.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three groupings: I. August 2000 Acquisition. II. August 2004 Acquisition. III. May 2008 Acquisition.
Material within this collection has been organized by acquisition reflecting the fact that the collection has been acquired in increments over time.

Researchers should note that material within each acquisition overlaps with and/or relates to material found in other acquisitions. For instance, correspondence and writings can be found in all three groupings. In order to locate all relevant material within this collection, researchers will need to consult each acquisition described in the Collection Contents section.
Researchers should also note that similar material can be arranged differently in each acquisition, depending on how the material was organized when it was received by the library.

did.unittitle: August 2000 Acquisition

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Ken Lopez Bookseller on the Eugene G. O'Neill Memorial Fund, 2000.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 44 (audio and video cassettes): Use of originals is restricted. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 45, 47 (reel-to-reel recording, video and film reels): Restricted Fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 46 (computer disks): Restricted Fragile. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.p: Includes some in boxes labeled "The Everlasting Sky" and some of James White Hawk and one of Sen. and Mrs. Hubert Humphrey "playing Indian"; one page of notes on White Hawk case.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Elisabeth Simonot, Trickster et Sang-Croise dans Trois Nouvelles de Gerald Vizenor (dissertation with letter to GV in English), "Water Striders (Haiku Poems)," and poetry pamphlet by Vizenor labeled "scarce," among other material.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Use of originals is restricted. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A biography on Gerald Vizenor is included on the back of the tape. It reads: His life, like his work, was a long time taking root in a place and a culture. Drawing on his Ojibwa heritage, the bitter effects of his father's murder when he was himself still a baby, his intermittent formal education, and his need to reconcile the tribal past with the political present, Vizenor has, poem by poem, story by story, and novel by novel constructed an impressive oeuvre that marks him as among the most prolific and most intellectually challenging writers of the Native American renaissance.

did.unittitle: "The Right to Identity," Part 1, Feb 1975, First Lecture at Berkeley
did.materialspec: This video is in poor condition. The video drops out throughout the tape. The sound is okay.

did.unittitle: "The Right to Identity," Part 2, Feb 1975, First Lecture at Berkeley
"The Right to Identity," Part 3, Feb 1975, First Lecture at Berkeley
did.materialspec: The tape was in poor condition. The video does drop out. The audio is okay. The video is in black and white.

did.unittitle: "The Red Coin" by Gerald Vizenor, Performed by Kim Blaeser's Literature Class

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A handwritten note, dated 1995-10-20, is included with the tape and reads: Hi Jerry, Thanks for the kind message you left about the book. It means a lot to me that you think it's good. And thanks for the copy of "The Writer's Notebook". You know you've made it when people want your bar napkins! I'm sending this tape one of my students made last spring. It's supposed to be a version of your story "The Red Coin". It's odd but you will probably get a kick out of it just the same. All is well here I go home for my Mom's tests next Wednesday. Send good healing energies. Just talked to Gordy and wondered when the three of us might even be in the same place. That would be "good fun" as my mom says. Anyway just wanted to let you know I'm thinking of you. Hi to Laura + Amy. Best, Kim Oh, I almost forgot: HAPPY BIRTHDAY!!

did.unittitle: "Trickster Chaos: Old Stories and New Science in the Postindian Novel" by Linda Lizut Helstern, typescript of an academic dissertation re: Gerald Vizenor and others, with cover letter, 216pp
"Dark River" by Louis Owens, typescript, 289pp
"Storied Voices in Native American Texts" by Blanca Chester, typescript of an academic dissertation, 262pp
"A Red Girl's Reasoning: Early Native American Women Writers and the Post-Reservation Home, 1883-1927" by Betty Louise Bell, typescript with cover letter, 181pp

"Writing Circles Around Indian Territory: the Selected Short Stories Cherokee Writer and Editor Ora Eddleman Reed" by Alexia Kosmider, typescript with cover letter, correspondence and photocopies, 616-pp
"Bringing Into Being" edited by Jacquelyn Kilpatrick, typescript of a projected book re: mixed blood novelist and scholar Louis Owens with cover letter and outline, 71pp
Literary and personal correspondence re: the death of mixed-blood novelist and scholar Louis Owens, who died of a self-inflicted gunshot wound in 2002, approx. 500pp
"The Mask Make" by Diane Glancy, typescript dated 9/12/00 and labeled "Revised copy #2" with cover letter, 203pp
"Designs of the Night Sky" by Diane Glancy, typescript, 148pp
Miscellaneous literary and academic submissions to the University of Oklahoma Press American Indian Literature and Critical Studies Series, approx. 200pp
Literary and business correspondence re: University of Oklahoma Press American Indian Literature and Critical Studies Series, plus promotional pamphlets, approx. 500pp
Royalty statements
"Hiroshima Bugi," one set of master page proofs with corrections, 209pp
"Hiroshima Bugi," typescript, 165pp
"Bear Island: The War at Sugar Point," typescript dated 2/04 with corrections, 43pp
"Bear Island: The War at Sugar Point," early typescript in a gray notebook dated 10/22/03 with heavy corrections
"Bear Island: The War at Sugar Point," early notebook of notes and prose drafts dated 1/00, 157pp
Research materials re: "Bear Island: The War at Sugar Point" and Sugar Point, approx. 200pp
Notebook: One composition book of holograph notes re: "Bear Island: The War at Sugar Point"
"Jeux de frontieres: L'ecriture poetique dans "Dead Voices" (1992) de Gerald Vizenor" by Najoua Hannachi-Gregolre, bound doctoral thesis re: Vizenor (In French, In three volumes), 795pp
"(Alter)Native Survivance: Postindian Comedy and Motion In Gerald Vizenor's "Hotline Healers" by Ying-wen Yu, bound academic thesis re: Vizenor, 86pp

"Literature as Weapon: How Three Native American Writers Have Fought to Sustain Their People's Identity and Presence" by Rob Murdoch, honors thesis (B.A.) re: Vizenor, Leslie Marmon Silko and Sherman Alexie, 48pp
"Photographers of Native Americans," bound group of seminar papers, Native American Studies, University of California Berkeley
Miscellaneous publications, pamphlets, brochures, P.R. materials etc. re: Vizenor and the University of Nebraska Press
Award
"Hiroshima Bugi: Atomu 57" ringbound manuscript, 271pp
"Jeux de frontieres: L'ecriture poetique dans "Dead Voices" (1992) de Gerald Vizenor" by Najoua Hannachi- Gregoire, doctoral thesis re: Vizenor (in French, in two volumes), 355pp
"Chancers," ring bound manuscript dated 8/99, 157pp
"Who Owns the Body?" international conference held at Berkeley in 9/00, correspondence and research material
"Fugitive Poses: Native American Scenes of Absence and Presence," page proofs of the Japanese translation, 426pp
Book, "Contemporary Authors/ Autobiography Series: Volume 22" (NY: Gale Research, 1996), includes Vizenor entry, 411 pp
Book, "Multiculturalism In Contemporary Societies: Perspectives on Difference and Transdifference" (Erlangen, GER: Erlangen, 2002), includes Vizenor essay, 272pp
Book, "Wordarrows: Native States of Literary Sovereignty" by Gerald Vizenor, first wrappers edition, 164pp
Book, "John Steinbeck: Centennial Reflections by American Writers," includes Vizenor essay, 115pp
"Interimage Simulations: Fugitive Poses of Native American Indians" by Gerald Vizenor, offprint (two copies), 18pp
"A Comprehensive Bibliography: Gerald Vizenor" by Jamie Bunch, 34pp
Miscellaneous publications, pamphlets, brochures, P.R. materials etc. re: Vizenor and the University of Nebraska Press
"Hiroshima Bugi: Atomu 57," corrected manuscript (October 2002), 271 pp
"Hiroshima Bugi: Atomu 57," page proofs dated 3-31-03, 210pp
"Hiroshima Bugi: Atomu 57," copy-edited typescript with minor corrections, plus cover letter, dated 12/26/02, 289pp
"Chancers" paperback edition, literary and business correspondence plus cover proofs, P.R. etc., approx. 25pp

Josef Raab Bielefeld Conference Oct 23-26 2002, "Mister Kennewick's Liberty: Truth Games and Native American Conference Oct 23-26 2002 "Sovereignty," variant drafts of lecture with correspondence, approx. 40pp

"Autobiography Essay," typescript for Gale Research Contemporary Authors book, plus additional biographical info.
"Wordarrows: Native States of Literary Sovereignty," manuscript with corrections and cover letter for the Bison Books paper edition

"Chancers: Selected Scenes from the Novel" by Gerald Vizenor from the book "Imaginary (Re-) Locations: Tradition, Modernity, and the Market in Contemporary Native American Literature and Culture" (Stauffenberg Verlag), offprint (four copies), 15pp

Business and literary correspondence

"Hiroshima Bugi: Atomu 57," first version dated 8/01 with heavy corrections and rewrites, 58pp

"Hiroshima Bugi: Atomu 57," early draft with numerous corrections, approx. 125pp
"Hiroshima Bugi: Atomu 57," early draft with alternate title of "Atomu 57: Hiroshima Kabuki" with numerous corrections
"Hiroshima Bugi: Atomu 57," early draft of Chapters 1-16 with numerous corrections, 150pp

"Hiroshima Bugi: Atomu 57," research material, approx. 100pp

"Hiroshima Bugi: Atomu 57," research material

"George Morrison: Anishinaabe Expressionism at Red Rock": variant manuscript drafts w/ corrections of this essay on artist George Morrison, plus research materials, approx. 500pp
July 2004 Essays, Lectures

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: "Crazy Papers" (7pp); "George Morrison" (21 pp); "Native American Narratives" (27pp); "Imagic Moments" (28pp); "Edward Curtis" (18pp); "Fusions of Survivance" (19pp); "Wordarrows" (15pp); "Mister Ishi" (16pp); "Trickster Hermeneutics" (10pp); "Mercenary Sovereignty" (17pp); "Native American Literature in the 1930s" (6pp); "Native American Survivance" (24pp); Santa Maria Casino" (64 pp)

Student Evaluations 1980s - 1990s, student evaluations re: University of California
Notebook, "January 1975 Notes," red notebook of holograph entries from January - April, 1975, approx. 70pp
Notebook, "1996 - Haiku Place Poems," composition notebook of holograph poetry entries dated June 4, 1991 - May 16, 1993, approx. 24pp
Notebook, "Plays Dec. 1999," composition notebook of holograph notes re: "The President's Teeth," 3pp
Notebook, "Commodity Art /Almost Series," composition notebook of holograph notes of story ideas, approx. 90pp
Notebook, "1984-1988": composition notebook of holograph notes dating from January 1984 - February 1991, approx. 180pp
Notebook, "Summer 1991-1995," composition notebook of holograph notes re: story, book and essay topics dating from June 6, 1991- 1995, 160pp
Notebook, "Manifest/Ishi", composition notebook of holograph notes re: Ishi, approx. 42pp
Notebook, "Jews and Indians 1996," composition notebook of holograph notes, approx. 16pp

Notebook, green cloth notebook of misc. holograph notes dating from 1994 and/or 1997, approx. 60pp
Notebook, black notebook of misc. holograph notes dating from Lake Forest, IL 1970 -1972, approx. 14pp
Notebook, untitled black notebook of misc. holograph notes dating from October 18, 1987 - May 1989, approx. 90pp
Notebook, "Point Roberts," small blue spiral notebook of misc. holograph notes, approx. 25pp

Notebook, "Psychotherapy": blue spiral notebook of notes re: "Psychotherapy" symposium, January 27-28, 1979, approx. 140pp
Notebook, "Ishi 1994," small blue notebook of notes re: "Ishi" dating from October 1981 - May 1994, 124pp
Notebook, "Notes 1983," one small blue notebook of notes from 1983, 37 pp
Notebook, "Summer 1992," one small blue record notebook of notes from June 18, 1992 to July 1993, approx. 80pp
Notebook, "1980," one small red record notebook of notes from May 1980 - January 19, 1995, approx. 40pp

Book, "The Everlasting Sky" by Gerald Vizenor (Minnesota Historical Society Press, 2000): paperback edition, 140pp
Book, "Cultural Encounters in the New World" (Gunter Narr Verlag, 2003): includes Vizenor's "Mercenary Sovereignty" essay, 457pp

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Use of originals is restricted. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A description of the cassette's contents notes: "A fusion of avant-garde jazz, rock and classical music, composed and arranged to support free improvisation on the electric, and electronically altered guitar."

did.unittitle: seventeen chirps / no swan so fine / spotted horses, Sol Krueger (2 of 2 audiocassettes)
Cover proofs from "Earthdivers: Tribal Narratives of Mixed Descent"
Illustrations of Native Americans

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: White Swan (Crow), Chief White Buffalo (Arapaho), Thunder Cloud (Blackfeet), Chief Wets It (Assiniboine), High Bear (Sioux), and Chief Red Cloud (Sioux)

did.unittitle: Miscellaneous publications with Gerald Vizenor articles
Photographs: Two 10" x 10" black and white photographs taken of Vizenor when he was in Germany in 2000
Poster: "The Story of A Distinguished Anishinaabe A Presentation by Gerald Vizenor: Expressionist Painter: George Morrison: A Presentation by Gerald Vizenor: February 26, 2004"
June 2008 Acquisition

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Ken Lopez Bookseller on the Thornton Wilder Memorial Fund, 2008.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five series: Correspondence, Writings, Writings by Others, Academic and Conference Material, and Other Papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This portion of the collection includes correspondence, writings, slides, photographs, clippings, audiovisual material, art work, a typewriter, computer disks, and other papers.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 102 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 80 (computer disks): Restricted Fragile. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Correspondence
Business and academic

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 1 computer disk

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Computer disk is restricted. Reference copies of electronic files are available through Access Services:
Disk #20080530-16443.0001; CD-R, "Thesis 7/9/2005 - Name: Dr. T. C. Brindhakumari"

did.unittitle: Business and academic
Business and academic
Business and academic
Center Genocide

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence and contacts concerning a proposed Genocide and Holocaust center.

did.unittitle: Carmen Manuel Catalan Translation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes business correspondence with Carmen Manuel regarding a Catalan translation of "Almost Ashore," as well as contracts and an introduction by Vizenor.

did.unittitle: Writings

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged largely in alphabetical order by title.

did.unittitle: The Aesthetics of Survivance: Literary Theory and Practice

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a draft of this lecture with corrections, given at the Nolte Center Institute of Advanced Study at the University of Minnesota on April 9, 2007

did.unittitle: Almost Ashore: Selected Poems, drafts, publisher's galleys, and related correspondence
Bear Island, drafts, publisher's proofs and galleys, and related correspondence
Bearheart, bound page proofs of the reprint edition
Curriculum vitae
Edward Curtis: Pictorialist and Ethnographic Adventurist
Essays

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photocopies of various essays, including "Wounded Knee: December 1890: The End of the Century," "Gerald Vizenor and A. Robert Lee in Conversation," "The Elevator Shaman," "Fusions of Survivance: Haiku Scenes and Native Dream Songs."

did.unittitle: "Father Meme"/"Panic Portage," drafts, publisher's proofs, and related correspondence
"Father Meme" / "Panic Portage"
Geneva/Vienna Lecture: "Native American Narratives: Resistance and Survivance"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes draft of keynote speech given at the Vienna Conference on November 17, 2006.

did.unittitle: Notebook: "Genocide"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes one holograph notebook regarding a graduate seminar on genocide, given at the University of New Mexico, in the Fall of 2006.

did.unittitle: Genocide Tribunals: Human Rights and Native Survivance, essay drafts, research materials, and related correspondence
George Morrison: Anishinaabe Expressionist Artist

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes numerous essay drafts with revisions and related correspondence, and three sheets of color slides of Morrison's work.

did.unittitle: Griever: An American Monkey King in China, bound page proofs of the reprint edition
Literary Chance: Essays on Native American Survivance, early drafts with corrections and additions
Native American Narratives: Resistance and Survivance

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a draft of Vizenor's chapter with corrections, dated February 24, 2005, as well as related correspondence.

did.unittitle: Native Liberty: Natural Reason and Cultural Survivance: Selected Essays

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a first draft with notes and corrections, a manuscript copy dated 2008, and a final copy dated July 23, 2007.

did.unittitle: Native American Literature

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes three variant drafts of an essay written for a Native American Literature catalog for bookseller Ken Lopez, dated August 2004, with related correspondence and a copy of the catalog.

did.unittitle: Native Provenance and Museum Collections

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes drafts, correspondence, and research for a lecture and panel discussion given at the National Museum of the American Indian.

did.unittitle: Native Survivance and Irony

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes drafts with corrections, correspondence, and research for a lecture given at the Bavarian American Studies Association in Munich, Germany, on May 19, 2006.

did.unittitle: New Mexico Lecture: "Survivance Narratives"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a draft, corrections, and correspondence for a keynote lecture given at the Indigenous Research Conference at the University of New Mexico, on April 3, 2008.

did.unittitle: Poetry

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes manuscripts of poems through 1980 with corrections and revisions.

did.unittitle: Poetry 2004

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes stray poem drafts with corrections.

did.unittitle: Postindian Reflections: Gerald Vizenor and A. Robert Lee in Conversation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes multiple drafts with corrections, plus related correspondence.

did.unittitle: The Progress and White Earth Survivance

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes two variant drafts and research dated April, 2006.

did.unittitle: Red River Conference

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence and notes concerning the Red River Conference on World Literature, held at North Dakota State University, Fargo, ND.

did.unittitle: Survivance: Narratives of Native Presence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes drafts with corrections, as well as general business correspondence and correspondence between essay contributors and the publisher.

did.unittitle: Syllabi

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes syllabi for courses in Native American Studies at the University of California, Berkeley, and elsewhere.

did.unittitle: We Are at Home

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes variant drafts with corrections, plus correspondence, for Vizenor's foreword to "We Are at Home," by Shannon Pennefeather.

did.unittitle: Western Literature Association Award Lecture

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes two variant drafts with corrections, as well as a conference program.

did.unittitle: Reviews, Hiroshima Bugi
Research materials
Dennis Banks -- Ojibwa Warrior

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains general research materials and legal records concerning Dennis Banks, as well as Vizenor's notes and correspondence.

did.unittitle: Father Meme

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a notebook of research materials on Catholic Church abuses of Native American children, as well as other research for "Father Meme."

did.unittitle: Red Lake

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains research, newspaper clippings, and correspondence concerning the Red Lake Reservation school shootings in March, 2005.

did.unittitle: Ward Churchill

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folders contain research and correspondence regarding the controversy surrounding the University of Colorado professor Ward Churchill.

did.unittitle: Writings by Others
Carson, Ben. "The 'Cosmopolitan Consciousness' of Gerald Vizenor and Native American Literary Separatism"
The Columbia History of American Indian Literatures of the United States, 1945 - Present

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains Chapter Three ("Cannons and Canonization," by Kimberly M. Blaeser) and Chapter Five ("Non-Fiction Writers," by David Murray). Edited by Eric Cheyfitz.

did.unittitle: Cox, James H. "Muting White Noise"
Dennis, Helen M. "A Crack in the Universe: Towards a Spatialized Poetics of Native American Literature," proposal
Garrait-Bourrier, Anne. "N. Scott Momaday, The Bear Man"
Helstern, Linda. "The Fractal Geometry of Bearheart: The Heirship Chronicles"
Huddleston, Jason T. "The Dialectic of Self: An Existential Reading of Identity in Selected Contemporary American Indian Literatures"
Kilpatrick, Alan. "The Wolf Stalker"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a partial manuscript, written under the pseudonym A.E. Blackwood, with a cover letter.

did.unittitle: Lockard, Joe. "Facing the Windigoo: Gerald Vizenor and Primo Levi"
Pettigrew, Dawn Karima. "The Marriage of Saints"
Pierotti, Raymond. "Sits Beside Him"
Schlesier, Sibylle. "Bill Red Hat: Cheyenne Storier"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains manuscripts of Schlesier's introduction and epilogue, plus correspondence.

did.unittitle: Taylor, Paul. "Introduction"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a manuscript of Taylor's introduction (to "Native American Literatures") on Bearheart, with Vizenor's suggestions, comments, and correspondence.

did.unittitle: Trickster of Liberty

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence and cover proof for "Trickster of Liberty," as well as the foreword to "Bear Island" by Jace Weaver.

did.unittitle: Weber, Ivan. "Coherent Incoherence: The Literature of Gerald Vizenor"
Academic and Conference Materials
Final Exams - Berkeley
Letters of Recommendation
Final Grades - Berkeley
Japan

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 1 computer disk

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, information, and itineraries concerning an Osaka University position; student papers from a seminar on Hiroshima, human rights, and genocide; and various manuscripts and lecture notes from Vizenor's series of Japan lectures from October, 2004.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Computer disk is restricted. Reference copies of electronic files are available through Access Services:
Disk #20080530-16443.0002; "Keio University Tokyo Lecture Oct 25, 2004 Photographs"

did.unittitle: American Studies New Mexico

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes syllabi, correspondence, and materials concerning Vizenor's American Studies courses at the University of New Mexico.

did.unittitle: Miscellaneous Academic and Conference Materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 1 computer disk

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes miscellaneous materials such as travel documents, brochures, and correspondence regarding various literary conferences, lectures, and trips. The contents include materials on the Monterey Institute (2007), the "American Aesthetics" conference (Geneva, 2006), the "Native American and First Nations" conference (Vienna, 2006), the Modern Library Association (Philadelphia, 2006), the University of Minnesota "Genocide Tribunals" (2006), Paris trips (2006-2007), the "Montpellier Symposium" (2007), and University of Minnesota tenure materials.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Computer disk is restricted. Reference copies of electronic files are available through Access Services:
Disk #20080530-16443.0003; "LSC WLSC Orientation"

did.unittitle: White Earth Reservation Constitutional Conference

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes business and organizational materials from the White Earth Reservation Constitutional Conference, October 19-20, 2007, plus correspondence and notes.

did.unittitle: University of New Mexico Press

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Univeristy of New Mexico Press Committee meeting memos and minutes.

did.unittitle: Other Papers
Publications

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 4 computer disks

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes miscellaneous publications: UNM Today, Fuse Magazine, European Review of Native American Studies, Anishinaabeg Today, PMLA, Ojibwe News, Minnesota History, Fiction Collective, etc.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Computer disks are restricted. Reference copies of electronic files are available through Access Services:
Disk #20080530-16443.0004; DVD, "American Genocide and Justice"
Disks #20080530-16443.0006, #20080530-16443.0007; CD (commercially mastered), "The Native American Journey"

did.unittitle: Travel Documents
Montpellier Conference

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains papers, travel documents, and correspondence concerning the "Gerald Vizenor, Litterateur at Large" International Symposium, held at Universite Paul Valery in Montpellier, France, in May of 2006.

did.unittitle: Montpellier Conference
Munich May 19, 2006
London, Paris, July 13-24, 2006
Legal Records
Highlands Club

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes documents relating to the case of "Gerald Vizenor v. Robin Mickle" regarding Highland Country Club (of Oakland, CA) fees.

did.unittitle: 742 Camino Francisca Complaint Umphrey

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes documents relating to the case of "Gerald Vizenor v. Michael Umphrey" regarding a Santa Fe home.

did.unittitle: Personal Effects

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a Patrick Henry Class of '53 50th Class Reunion brochure (2003), one year planner (2003), a reproduction of Pavel Cerenkov's I.D. from the National Atomic Museum, three name tags (2006), two pocket diaries (2006-2007), one international driving permit (2007), and business papers regarding a 2007 house purchase.

did.unittitle: Miscellaneous

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 1 computer disk

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Use of originals is restricted. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information:
Disk #20080530-16443.0008; "The Complete Adventures of Jimby the Lion"

did.unittitle: Robert Lee's lecture on Gerald Vizenor, given at the Universite Paul Valery in Montpellier, France

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Use of originals is restricted. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Broadside: Those Stubborn Flies

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of one framed haiku broadside published by Woodland Pattern bookstore, Milwaukee.

did.unittitle: Distinguished Minnesotan Award
American Book Award
Book by Miyuki Aozama (on Native American Literature and a holograph cover letter dated June 6, 2004)
Bemidji State University Honorary Alumnus Award
Bemidji State University Distinguished Minnesotan Award
Western Literature Association's Distinguished Achievement Award
Posters and Original Art

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a "Black Arrow" movie poster (circa 1944), a "My Chippewa" sheet music (circa 1907), a "Sacred Trusts" drawings, and a watercolor painting.

did.unittitle: Vizenor Posters
Posters and Original Art

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a "Kalispell I" print, a Jaune Quick-to-See Smith poster, and a "Wordarrows Autographing Party with Gerald Vizenor" poster.

did.unittitle: Original cover art for "Shadow Distance", by Dirk Görtler
Photographs, posters and poetry broadsides

did.unittitle: Object
Typewriter

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gerald Vizenor's Smith Corona manual typewriter in a case with a cloth flag/map of Sicily laid within.
